summary: Joel Spolsky announces that Anil Dash is becoming the new CEO of Fog Creek Software, the company Joel co-founded sixteen years earlier. Joel explains that he and co-founder Michael Pryor are now fully occupied running Stack Overflow and Trello respectively, necessitating new leadership. He emphasizes that Anil's deep understanding of developers and technology ethics makes him the right fit for Fog Creek's unique culture. The company is focusing on FogBugz and its new product Gomix (formerly HyperDev), a developer playground for building web apps. Joel frames Fog Creek not as a single-product startup but as an enduring place where great people build great things together.
key_points: Anil Dash becomes Fog Creek's first outside CEO in the company's sixteen-year history, chosen for his alignment with the company's unique values and culture
Joel Spolsky is now full-time CEO of Stack Overflow (300 employees) and Michael Pryor is full-time CEO of Trello (100 employees), leaving Fog Creek needing dedicated leadership
Fog Creek distinguishes itself from typical startups by being built around people and culture rather than a single product, which Joel argues makes it fundamentally more durable
The company is focused on two products: the established FogBugz developer tool and the newly relaunched Gomix (formerly HyperDev) for rapid full-stack web app development
money_quotes: There is no single person I would trust more to help Fog Creek figure out how to make something big, important, and valuable.
A typical startup is built around a single product, and some theory that people will pay money for that product. This theory eventually become false, and the company goes away.
We were just a place where great people come together to build great things.
People come here because of the other people that are here. And that makes it fundamentally much stronger and longer lasting.

We would never dream of having just any competent person from outside the company come in, let alone give them the CEO role, if we weren't convinced that they were 100% fanatical and excited about Fog Creek Software's unique operating system.
As long as we remain dedicated to being a place where great people come together to build great things we're going to remain a highly respected and successful company for a long long time.
key_insight: A company built around attracting great people rather than a single product thesis is fundamentally more durable, because it can continuously generate new products even as individual ones succeed or fail.
